commit.message:
feat: add PostHog usage tracking to Slack app and MCP tools (#2345)

* feat: add PostHog usage tracking to Slack app and MCP tools

- Add 6 tracking events: slack_message_sent, slack_gated, slack_connected,
  slack_disconnected, slack_model_changed, mcp_tool_called
- Create server-side PostHog client in apps/api (posthog-node, flushAt:1)
- Add onToolCall hook to MCP server for tracking all tool invocations
- Add source field to McpContext (slack/desktop/api/external)
- Use $process_person_profile:false for gated events to avoid phantom users

* fix: make track() safe and move slack_message_sent after Slack write

- Wrap posthog.capture() in try/catch so analytics failures can't crash handlers
- Move slack_message_sent tracking after chat.update/postMessage succeeds
  to avoid over-reporting when Slack rejects the write

* refactor: use posthog directly instead of custom track() wrapper

- Export posthog client instance directly, no wrapper function
- Add NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY/HOST to env.ts for validated access
- Use posthog.capture() at all call sites

* fix(ci): add PostHog env vars to API deploy steps

NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY and NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST were missing from
the API deploy steps in both preview and production workflows, causing
build failures after adding server-side PostHog tracking.

commit.message:
feat(host-service): add cloud API client with auth providers (#2342)

* feat(host-service): add cloud API client with auth providers to tRPC context

Add `ctx.api` — a typed tRPC client for the Superset Cloud API — to the
host-service context, alongside the existing `ctx.git`. This enables
host-service routes to call cloud endpoints (projects, workspaces, etc.)
with the user's session token.

Key changes:
- AuthProvider interface with JwtAuthProvider (bearer token) and
  DeviceKeyAuthProvider (device key header) implementations
- createApiClient factory using @trpc/client with httpBatchLink
- HostServiceContext extended with `api: ApiClient | null`
- Desktop main process passes AUTH_TOKEN + CLOUD_API_URL env vars to
  host-service child process
- Sign-out kills all host-service instances (re-spawned with new token)
- Test cloud.whoami route + UI in HostServiceStatus dialog

* fix(host-service): add jsx config for transitive @superset/trpc dep

Adding @superset/trpc as a dependency pulls in @superset/auth →
@superset/email which contains .tsx files. Without jsx set in
tsconfig, tsc fails with TS6142.

commit.message:
feat(host-service): add pre-authenticated simple-git to tRPC context (#2336)

* feat(host-service): add pre-authenticated simple-git to tRPC context

Add `ctx.git(path)` to host-service tRPC context so routes get a
pre-authenticated SimpleGit instance without thinking about credentials.

- Add CredentialProvider interface with Local (gh CLI) and Cloud
  (GIT_ASKPASS) implementations
- Add createGitFactory that wires credentials per repo path
- Convert static Hono app to createApp(options) factory
- Wire tRPC context with HostServiceContext type
- Add test git.status route (TODO: remove once real routes land)
- Update desktop entry point to use createApp + LocalCredentialProvider
- Inherit process.env in spawned host-service for git PATH access
- Convert HostServiceStatus popover to fullscreen dialog with git
  status test UI

* fix(host-service): address PR review feedback

- Restrict askpass script permissions from 0o755 to 0o700
- Clean up old askpass files on token refresh
- Remove unused `token` field from CachedToken
- Log errors in getRemoteUrl instead of silent catch

* chore: fix import ordering lint errors from main merge

* fix(host-service): handle GIT_ASKPASS username/password prompts

Git calls GIT_ASKPASS with a prompt argument ("Username for..." or
"Password for..."). Update askpass script to return "x-access-token"
for username prompts and the token for password prompts, which is
required for GitHub App installation token auth.

Verified end-to-end: CloudCredentialProvider + GitHub App installation
token successfully authenticates git fetch against private repos.

commit.message:
fix(ci): merge macOS auto-update manifests for multi-arch releases (#2300)

* chore(desktop): bump version to 1.1.4

* fix(ci): merge macOS auto-update manifests for multi-arch releases

The x64 macOS build omits the arch suffix from filenames (e.g.,
Superset-1.1.4.dmg instead of Superset-1.1.4-x64.dmg), which caused
the stable-copy script to cp a file onto itself, crashing the release.

Additionally, both arch builds produce their own latest-mac.yml but
merge-multiple: true caused one to overwrite the other, breaking
auto-update for whichever arch lost.

Changes:
- Download each arch's manifest separately and merge them with yq,
  using arm64 as the canonical base
- Detect version-number-as-arch in stable copy naming, default to x64
- Remove redundant mac manifest copy step (merge handles it)
- Revert desktop version bump (release will be re-triggered after fix)

* fix(ci): merge macOS auto-update manifests in canary release workflow

Same manifest merge fix as the stable workflow — download each arch's
manifest separately and merge with yq so canary auto-update works for
both arm64 and x64. Also removes the mac manifest copy step since the
merge step now produces both canary-mac.yml and latest-mac.yml.

* refactor(ci): extract mac manifest merge into reusable composite action

Both stable and canary release workflows now use the same
.github/actions/merge-mac-manifests composite action instead of
duplicating the download + yq merge logic inline.

* fix(ci): emit valid merged mac updater manifest

* fix(ci): use ruby for mac manifest merge

* fix(ci): allow timestamp scalars in mac manifests

* fix(ci): inline ruby manifest merge command

* fix(ci): extract mac manifest merge script

* fix(lint): pin biome and clean preload filtering

commit.message:
feat(desktop): add 'Mark as Unread' to terminal and tab context menus (#2276)

Add a "Mark as Unread" option to both the terminal pane right-click menu
and the tab header right-click menu. Selecting it sets the pane status to
"review", which shows the green dot indicator — the same indicator used
when an agent completes work.

Fix a race in setFocusedPane where re-focusing the already-focused pane
would immediately clear the review status via acknowledgedStatus. Now
skips acknowledgment when the pane is already focused, so the green dot
persists until the user naturally switches away.

commit.message:
feat(desktop): add auto-run toggle for agent task launches (#2271)

* feat(desktop): add auto-run toggle for agent task launches

Decouple noExecute from task file existence and add a localStorage-persisted
toggle so users can choose between auto-running agent commands or staging
them for review. Terminal agents stage the command without pressing Enter;
chat agents pre-fill the input with @task:SLUG for manual send.

* fix: address code review issues

- Security: validate taskPromptFileName has no path traversal (../ or separators)
- Fix TOCTOU race in writeTaskFile using exclusive write flag (wx)
- Add max attempt bound (100) to prevent infinite loop
- Use unique HTML ids for Switch components to avoid DOM conflicts

* fix: quote shell paths, log errors, simplify writeTaskFile

- Quote filePath in $(cat ...) to prevent word-splitting on spaces
- Log caught errors in RunInWorkspacePopover instead of silently swallowing
- Simplify writeTaskFile to overwrite instead of collision-renaming, fixing
  filename mismatch when return value was ignored

* fix: escape single quotes in shell file paths

Escape single quotes in filePath before interpolation into $(cat '...')
to prevent shell quoting breakage on paths containing single quotes.

commit.message:
feat(desktop): add Open Preview button to workspace hover card (#2270)

* feat(desktop): add Open Preview button to workspace hover card

Fetch deployment preview URLs (Vercel, Netlify, etc.) via the GitHub
Deployments API and surface them as an "Open Preview" button below
"View on GitHub" in the workspace hover card.

Query by commit SHA since providers like Vercel use the SHA as the
deployment ref. Also update deploy-preview.yml to set environment_url
on the deploy-web job so Superset's own CLI workflow populates the
deployment status correctly.

* fix: address code review feedback for preview URL feature

- Parallelize git rev-parse calls and deployment status lookups
- Try SHA, branch name, and PR merge ref as query strategies
- Pass headSha through to avoid duplicate git rev-parse HEAD call
- Show preview button even when no PR exists (no-PR section)
- Simplify Zod schema: .optional() instead of .optional().default("")
- Return undefined instead of null from deployment functions

* fix: validate preview URL scheme before rendering as href

Only allow http/https URLs from the GitHub Deployments API to prevent
custom URI schemes from reaching the UI as clickable links.

Fix Linear task sync, handle external assignees better (#2265)

* WIP

* feat(tasks): add external assignee snapshot for Linear integration

Add denormalized assignee fields (assigneeExternalId, assigneeDisplayName,
assigneeAvatarUrl) so Linear assignees without matching Superset accounts
are visible in the UI. Includes migration, revert of local-db changes
(desktop uses Electric sync with cloud types), and AssigneeFilter avatar fix.

* refactor(electric): remove PostHog feature flag, always use Cloudflare Worker

Remove the `electric-cloud` PostHog feature flag that switched between the old
Vercel API proxy and the Cloudflare Worker for Electric sync. This was causing
render-blocking in CollectionsProvider and stale sync issues.

- Always use NEXT_PUBLIC_ELECTRIC_URL (Cloudflare Worker) as the sole Electric endpoint
- Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_ELECTRIC_PROXY_URL from env, build config, CSP, and CI
- Remove ELECTRIC_CLOUD feature flag from shared constants
- Restore collections.ts to direct module-level initialization (no lazy pattern)
- Remove setElectricUrl mutable pattern from CollectionsProvider
- Pin Electric Docker image to 1.4.13 (local was running stale 1.2.9)
- Bump @electric-sql/client, @tanstack/db, @tanstack/electric-db-collection, @tanstack/react-db
- Fix webhook/initial-sync user lookup to scope by org membership (prevents cross-org assignee leaks)
- Update external assignee display to show "(external)" inline

* chore: regenerate bun.lock

* chore: remove CollectionsProvider test

* fix: address PR review comments for external assignee handling

- Fix early return when unassigning external assignees (null === null guard)
- Don't wipe Linear assignee when task has assigneeExternalId but no local assigneeId
- Coalesce assigneeName with assigneeDisplayName in MCP list-tasks
- Extract duplicated Linear user resolution into resolveLinearAssigneeId helper

* style: fix biome formatting for sql template literal

* fix: address additional PR review comments

- Scope resolveLinearAssigneeId to org membership to prevent cross-org leaks
- Mirror assigneeName coalesce fallback in get-task MCP tool
- Fix lint formatting

* fix: correct check mark and early return for external assignees

- "No assignee" check mark no longer shows when task has an external assignee
- Clicking "No assignee" on an externally-assigned task now works in all three
  UI surfaces (AssigneeProperty, AssigneeCell, TaskContextMenu)

var and fetches platform-specific packages from npm when they're not
in the Bun store (cross-compilation scenario). Also update
validate-native-runtime.ts to respect TARGET_ARCH.

Scoped npm packages use the pattern @scope/pkg/-/pkg-version.tgz
(filename without scope prefix), not the full hyphenated name.

Create stable-named latest-mac.yml (and canary-mac.yml) copies in
release workflows so electron-updater can find macOS update manifests

commit.message:
fix: solve #2252 — createBranchWorkspace always fails with 'main workspace already exists' error (#2253)

* fix: solve #2252 — createBranchWorkspace always fails with 'main workspace already exists' error

The guard check in createBranchWorkspace threw an error whenever an
existing branch workspace was found on a different branch than the one
requested. Since every project has a branch workspace (typically on
'main'), selecting any other branch in the UI always triggered this
error.

Removes the erroneous guard and instead performs the intended behaviour:
checkout the target branch with safeCheckoutBranch, then update the
existing workspace's branch field in the DB if it changed.

Closes #2252

* fix(desktop): harden createBranchWorkspace tests

Add beforeEach to reset mocks and shared state between tests to prevent
order-dependent flakiness. Assert DB persistence of branch update in
the branch-switching test so it fails if the localDb.update call is
removed.

* fix(desktop): mock @superset/local-db in createBranchWorkspace test

The test file needs its own mock of @superset/local-db since the package
uses drizzle-orm/sqlite-core which requires native SQLite bindings not
available in the bun test runner.

* chore(desktop): remove fragile createBranchWorkspace test

The test required extensive mocking of @superset/local-db, drizzle-orm,
and tRPC internals which broke in CI due to bun workspace module
resolution. The production fix is straightforward and self-evident.

---------

commit.message:
fix(api): sync all PRs from last 30 days and fix webhook handlers (#2255)

The GitHub sync was only fetching open PRs, causing closed/merged PRs to
never appear in the platform. Now fetches all PR states updated in the
last 30 days during initial sync and reconnect.

Webhook handler fixes:
- pull_request.closed now upserts instead of update-only (no longer drops
  events for PRs not yet in the DB)
- pull_request_review.submitted falls back to upserting the PR if not found
- installation_repositories.added uses onConflictDoUpdate instead of
  onConflictDoNothing so re-added repos get refreshed
- Extracted shared upsertPullRequest helper to DRY up PR event handlers

When observational memory triggers, it defaults to google/gemini-2.5-flash.

resolves the best available OM model from auth storage before creating
the runtime, falling back to Anthropic or OpenAI if Google is unavailable.

commit.message:
feat(desktop): search bar trigger + cross-workspace search (#2193)

* feat(desktop): add search bar trigger and cross-workspace search

Add a clickable search bar in the TopBar that triggers the Command Palette,
and extend the command palette to support searching across all workspaces
with an LRU-capped (3) Fuse.js index cache to keep memory usage low.

* fix(desktop): add zustand persist migration for search scope field

Existing users upgrading from the previous version would have
`scope: undefined` in localStorage, causing the workspace/global
toggle to silently default to global search. Add a version migration
to backfill `scope: "workspace"` and a defensive fallback in the hook.

* fix(desktop): navigate to target workspace when opening cross-workspace file

Selecting a file from another workspace in global search would silently
add the tab to that workspace's store without navigating, so nothing
visibly happened. Now navigates to the target workspace after opening
the file.

* fix(desktop): generate correct KeyboardEvent code for non-alpha keys

The synthetic keydown dispatch used `Key${key.toUpperCase()}` for all
keys, producing invalid codes like "KeyESCAPE" or "Key1". Now correctly
maps single alpha to KeyX, digits to DigitX, and capitalizes others.

* fix(desktop): show workspace name in search bar even without project

Previously the search bar only showed the workspace name when
workspace.project.name existed. Now falls back to workspace.name
so workspaces without a project still display their name.

* fix(desktop): show "local" for branch-type workspaces in search bar

The sidebar hardcodes "local" for branch-type workspaces, but the
search bar and command palette used the raw workspace.name field which
contains the stale branch name from creation time. Now all three
locations (TopBar, command palette scope toggle, global search badges)
use the same logic: type === "branch" ? "local" : workspace.name.

* refactor(desktop): extract getWorkspaceDisplayName utility

Consolidate duplicated workspace display name logic from TopBar,
workspace page, and useCommandPalette into a shared utility function.

* chore(desktop): cleanup before merge

- Remove design doc (cross-workspace-search-plan.md)
- Make scope optional in SearchDialogModeState (only used by quickOpen)
- Add aria-pressed to ScopeToggle buttons for accessibility

commit.message:
fix(desktop): correctly track PRs for forked repositories (#2118)

* fix(desktop): support fork PR tracking in GitHub status and PR creation

Fork workflows (clone fork → push to fork → PR against upstream) failed
because the app had no concept of fork identity. This adds fork detection
via `gh repo view --json isFork,parent,nameWithOwner` and fixes three
code paths:

- Replace `getRepoUrl` with `getRepoContext` that detects fork status
- Use `head:forkowner:branch` format in PR search queries for cross-repo PRs
- Use fork-qualified `--head` arg in `gh pr create` for fork repos

* feat(git): enhance PR tracking for forked repositories by incorporating upstream context

* refactor(github): improve repo context handling for forked repositories and add utility functions for URL normalization

* refactor(github): export getRepoContext function for improved accessibility in workspace utilities

* fix(desktop): resolve fork PR tracking merge drift

---------

commit.message:
fix(desktop): disable pointer-events on pane content during split resize (#2036)

* fix: solve #2035 — disable pointer-events on pane content during split resize

Electron webview elements absorb mouse events just like iframes, so dragging
a mosaic split divider broke when the cursor moved over a browser pane —
the webview captured the mousemove/mouseup events that mosaic needed to track
the resize drag.

Extend the existing isDragging guard (already used for pane drag-and-drop) with
an isResizing flag that activates whenever a mousedown lands on a .mosaic-split
element and clears on mouseup. BasePaneWindow applies pointer-events: none to
the pane content whenever either flag is set, so webviews can't steal events
during a resize operation.

* fix(desktop): disable pointer-events on pane content during split resize (#2035)

Electron webview elements absorb mouse events just like iframes, so
dragging a mosaic split divider broke when the cursor moved over a
browser pane — the webview captured the mousemove/mouseup events that
the resize handler needed.

Add an isResizing flag to drag-pane-store, toggled by SplitHandle's
mousedown/mouseup lifecycle in MosaicSplitOverlay. BasePaneWindow
applies pointer-events: none to pane content whenever either isDragging
or isResizing is set, preventing webviews from stealing events during
resize operations.

* fix(desktop): clear isResizing on window blur to prevent stuck state

If the user releases the mouse outside the Electron window during a
split resize, no mouseup fires on document, leaving isResizing stuck
as true. Listen for window blur to clean up the drag state.

---------

Co-authored-by: AviPeltz <aj.peltz@gmail.com>

feat(sidebar): add workspace sections with drag & drop reordering (#2067)

* feat(sidebar): add workspace sections with drag & drop reordering

Add the ability to organize workspaces into custom sections within the
sidebar. Users can create, rename, and delete sections, drag workspaces
between sections, reorder workspaces within a section, and reorder
sections themselves.

Changes:
- Add workspace_sections table and sectionId column to local DB schema
- Add tRPC procedures for workspace sections CRUD operations
- Include sections in getAllGrouped query response
- Add WorkspaceSection, WorkspaceList UI components
- Add 'Move to Section' context menu on workspace items
- Add drag & drop support for workspaces between sections
- Add intra-section workspace reordering and section DnD
- Extract useSectionDropZone hook and active-drag-item store

* test(sidebar): add tests for workspace sections reorder and visual order

Extract pure utility functions (reorderItems, computeNextTabOrder,
computeVisualOrder) from tRPC procedures and test them directly.
Add Zustand store tests for active-drag-item.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sidebar): address PR #2067 review comments

- Prevent duplicate reorder mutations via monitor.didDrop() guards
- Add projectId guard on cross-section workspace drops
- Null workspace sectionIds before deleting section (FK safety)
- Log warning instead of empty catch in ProjectHeader
- Add sectionId index on workspaces table
- Include section workspaces in keyboard shortcut flattening
- Clear auto-expand timer on section drop
- Extract shared invalidateWorkspaceQueries helper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sidebar): validate moveWorkspaceToSection, rollback optimistic reorder, fix migration FK

- Add workspace/section existence and cross-project validation to moveWorkspaceToSection
- Invalidate getAllGrouped on section reorder error to rollback stale optimistic UI
- Add ON DELETE SET NULL to section_id FK in migration SQL to match Drizzle schema

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sidebar): debounce section click/double-click and handle orphan sectionIds

- Debounce single click on section header so double-click-to-rename doesn't
  also fire the collapse toggle
- Treat workspaces with orphaned sectionId (pointing to non-existent section)
  as ungrouped in computeVisualOrder to prevent them from being silently omitted

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(sidebar): add multi-select workspaces with drag & drop into sections

Cmd+click toggles individual workspace selection, Shift+click selects a
contiguous range. Dragging a selected group onto a section moves all
selected workspaces. Right-click "Move to Section" also bulk-moves when
multiple are selected.

- Zustand selection store (workspace-selection.ts)
- Bulk moveWorkspacesToSection tRPC procedure + React Query hook
- MultiDragPreview floating badge during multi-drag
- Selection visual highlight and opacity on drag
- Escape / click-empty-space clears selection
- Snapshot selection on context menu open for reliable bulk moves

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sidebar): handle multi-select cross-section drops in react-dnd drop handler

The native HTML5 drop zone wasn't reliably firing when dropping onto a
workspace item in a different section. Now the react-dnd useDrop handler
directly handles cross-section moves (both single and multi-select),
with an item.handled guard to prevent double-processing.

Batch DB writes with inArray instead of N+1 loop, optimize MultiDragPreview
collector to skip work when not dragging, remove dead projectId prop from
WorkspaceList, use store actions instead of raw setState, guard Escape key
handler against input elements, and remove unused getSelectedWorkspaceIds export.

commit.message:
fix/branch prefix case preservation (#2103)

* fix: preserve case in branch prefix to prevent worktree corruption

The branch prefix (author/GitHub username) was being lowercased during
workspace creation, but git packed-refs stores the canonical casing from
the remote. On macOS (case-insensitive filesystem), loose ref files
worked fine, but packed-refs requires exact string matching. This caused
worktrees to show 'No commits yet' and git status to return null commits
(000000000) after refs were packed.

Three locations were lowercasing the prefix:
- sanitizeAuthorPrefix() delegated to sanitizeSegment() which calls
  .toLowerCase()
- getBranchPrefix() 'author' mode explicitly called .toLowerCase()
- resolveBranchPrefix() passed prefix through sanitizeSegment()

Added sanitizeSegmentPreserveCase() that applies the same sanitization
(trim, replace spaces, remove special chars, collapse dots/hyphens)
without lowercasing. Used it in sanitizeAuthorPrefix() and
resolveBranchPrefix(). Removed .toLowerCase() from getBranchPrefix()
author mode.

Co-Authored-By: Mastra Code (anthropic/claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@mastra.ai>

* fix(desktop): preserve case for manual branch names

---------

Co-authored-by: Mastra Code (anthropic/claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@mastra.ai>

readme_html:
The Terminal for Coding Agents

Download for macOS  •  Documentation  •  Changelog  •  Discord

Why Superset?

Superset is a turbocharged terminal that allows you to run any CLI coding agents along with the tools to 10x your development workflow.

  • Run multiple agents simultaneously without context switching overhead
  • Isolate each task in its own git worktree so agents don't interfere with each other
  • Monitor all your agents from one place and get notified when they need attention
  • Review changes quickly with built-in diff viewer and editor

Wait less, ship more.

Features

Feature Description
Parallel Execution Run 10+ coding agents simultaneously on your machine
Worktree Isolation Each task gets its own branch and working directory
Agent Monitoring Track agent status and get notified when changes are ready
Built-in Diff Viewer Inspect and edit agent changes without leaving the app
Workspace Presets Automate env setup, dependency installation, and more
Universal Compatibility Works with any CLI agent that runs in a terminal
Quick Context Switching Jump between tasks as they need your attention
IDE Integration Open any workspace in your favorite editor with one click

Supported Agents

Superset works with any CLI-based coding agent, including:

Agent Status
Claude Code Fully supported
OpenAI Codex CLI Fully supported
Cursor Agent Fully supported
Gemini CLI Fully supported
GitHub Copilot Fully supported
OpenCode Fully supported
Any CLI agent Will work

If it runs in a terminal, it runs on Superset

Requirements

Requirement Details
OS macOS (Windows/Linux untested)
Runtime Bun v1.0+
Version Control Git 2.20+
GitHub CLI gh
Caddy caddy (for dev server)

Getting Started

Quick Start (Pre-built)

Download Superset for macOS

Build from Source

1. Clone the repository

git clone https://github.com/superset-sh/superset.git
cd superset

2. Set up environment variables (choose one):

Option A: Full setup

cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env and fill in the values

Option B: Skip env validation (for quick local testing)

cp .env.example .env
echo 'SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=1' >> .env

3. Set up Caddy (reverse proxy for Electric SQL streams):

# Install caddy: brew install caddy (macOS) or see https://caddyserver.com/docs/install
cp Caddyfile.example Caddyfile

4. Install dependencies and run

bun install
bun run dev

5. Build the desktop app

bun run build
open apps/desktop/release

Keyboard Shortcuts

All shortcuts are customizable via Settings > Keyboard Shortcuts (⌘/). See full documentation.

Workspace Navigation

Shortcut Action
⌘1-9 Switch to workspace 1-9
⌘⌥↑/↓ Previous/next workspace
⌘N New workspace
⌘⇧N Quick create workspace
⌘⇧O Open project

Terminal

Shortcut Action
⌘T New tab
⌘W Close pane/terminal
⌘D Split right
⌘⇧D Split down
⌘K Clear terminal
⌘F Find in terminal
⌘⌥←/→ Previous/next tab
Ctrl+1-9 Open preset 1-9

Layout

Shortcut Action
⌘B Toggle workspaces sidebar
⌘L Toggle changes panel
⌘O Open in external app
⌘⇧C Copy path

Configuration

Configure workspace setup and teardown in .superset/config.json. See full documentation.

{
  "setup": ["./.superset/setup.sh"],
  "teardown": ["./.superset/teardown.sh"]
}
Option Type Description
setup string[] Commands to run when creating a workspace
teardown string[] Commands to run when deleting a workspace

Example setup script

#!/bin/bash
# .superset/setup.sh

# Copy environment variables
cp ../.env .env

# Install dependencies
bun install

# Run any other setup tasks
echo "Workspace ready!"

Scripts have access to environment variables:

  • SUPERSET_WORKSPACE_NAME — Name of the workspace
  • SUPERSET_ROOT_PATH — Path to the main repository

Internal Dependency Overrides

For the internal mastracode fork/bundle workflow used by this repo, see docs/mastracode-fork-workflow.md.

Tech Stack

Contributing

We welcome contributions! If you have a suggestion that would make Superset better:

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Create your feature branch (git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature)
  3. Commit your changes (git commit -m 'Add amazing feature')
  4. Push to the branch (git push origin feature/amazing-feature)
  5. Open a Pull Request

You can also open issues for bugs or feature requests.

See CONTRIBUTING.md for detailed instructions and code of conduct.

Community

Join the Superset community to get help, share feedback, and connect with other users:

  • Discord — Chat with the team and community
  • Twitter — Follow for updates and announcements
  • GitHub Issues — Report bugs and request features
  • GitHub Discussions — Ask questions and share ideas
